methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createForAssemblyContext
methods.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.

methods.name: setMotionData
methods.description: Method that sets the motion data.

properties.name: assemblyContext
properties.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this MotionLink.

properties.name: entityToken
properties.description: Returns a token for the MotionLink object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same MotionLink.   When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

properties.name: errorOrWarningMessage
properties.description: Returns the error or warning message in the case where the healthState property returns either WarningFeatureHealthState or ErrorFeatureHealthState. Otherwise this property returns an empty string.

properties.name: healthState
properties.description: Returns the current health state of the MotionLink.

properties.name: isReversed
properties.description: Gets and sets whether the motion is reversed or not.

properties.name: isSuppressed
properties.description: Gets and sets if this MotionLink is suppressed.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: jointOne
properties.description: Gets the first joint for this MotionLink.

properties.name: jointTwo
properties.description: Gets the second joint for this MotionLink. This can return null if the linked motions are from the same joint.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets and sets the name of the MotionLink.

properties.name: nativeObject
properties.description: The NativeObject is the object outside the context of an assembly and in the context of it's parent component. Returns null in the case where this object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentComponent
properties.description: Returns the parent component that owns this MotionLink.

properties.name: timelineObject
properties.description: Returns the timeline object associated with this MotionLink.

properties.name: valueOne
properties.description: Returns the ModelParameter for the first motion link value.

properties.name: valueTwo
properties.description: Returns the ModelParameter for the second motion link value.

Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

classType.title: MotionLink.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.MotionLink.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::MotionLink::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createForAssemblyContext.title: MotionLink.createForAssemblyContext Method
createForAssemblyContext.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.

syntax.python: returnValue = motionLink_var.createForAssemblyContext(occurrence)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = motionLink_var->createForAssemblyContext(occurrence);

createForAssemblyContext.return_description: Returns the proxy object or null if this isn't the NativeObject.

parameters.name: occurrence
parameters.description: The occurrence that defines the context to create the proxy in.

deleteMe.title: MotionLink.deleteMe Method

syntax.python: returnValue = motionLink_var.deleteMe()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = motionLink_var->deleteMe();

deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the delete is successful.

setMotionData.title: MotionLink.setMotionData Method
setMotionData.description: Method that sets the motion data.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = motionLink_var.setMotionData(motionOne, valueOne, motionTwo, valueTwo)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = motionLink_var.setMotionData(motionOne, valueOne, motionTwo, valueTwo, isReversed)

parameters.name: motionOne
parameters.description: Specifies the first motion to link.

parameters.name: valueOne
parameters.description: Specifies the first motion link value. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters for length and radians for angle. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in" or "60 deg") or if no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length or angle.

parameters.name: motionTwo
parameters.description: Specifies the second motion to link.

parameters.name: valueTwo
parameters.description: Specifies the second motion link value. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters for length and radians for angle. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in" or "60 deg") or if no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length or angle.

parameters.name: isReversed
parameters.description: Optional argument that specifies whether to reverse the direction of the motion.   This is an optional argument whose default value is False.
